model.label: GPT-5.4
model.description: OpenAI • Frontier GPT model for general-purpose reasoning, tool use, and agentic workflows.

pick.rationale: UConn is the clear pick because this matchup shows a major class gap: Furman rates around No. 191-195 in KenPom and Torvik, while UConn is seeded on the 2-line and is also backed by a market spread of roughly -20.5. Furman has a theoretical upset path if it turns the game into a low-possession shooting contest, but its below-average offensive and defensive efficiency leave very little margin against a much more talented, higher-ceiling opponent. I did not find a meaningful availability concern strong enough to justify fading the Huskies.

reasoningStep.title: UConn over Furman
reasoningStep.summary: UConn is the safer and overwhelmingly more likely winner based on the large team-quality gap, strong market support, and no major injury news changing the baseline outlook.
evidence: Furman rates about No. 195 in Torvik and No. 191 in KenPom, with adjusted efficiency numbers near national-average or worse on both ends.
Web matchup coverage listed UConn as roughly a 20.5-point favorite with a -5000 moneyline, indicating a very low upset probability.
ESPN and web searches did not surface a significant UConn availability issue that would materially narrow the gap.
Furman's credible path is variance through pace and shooting, but its underlying profile suggests that path is narrow against a 2-seed opponent.

toolCalls.summary: Returned combined Torvik/KenPom ratings for 2 teams.

result.text: UConn vs Furman picks, predictions, odds for March Madness NCAA game Skip to main content Home Schedule Football Basketball Big Ten Sports Odds Newsletter College Sports Wire USA TODAY Sports UConn vs Furman picks, predictions, odds for NCAA March Madness game Jeremy Cluff Arizona Republic Show Caption Hide Caption March Madness Final Four predictions and national champion picks From chalk favorites to hot upsets, the panel shares Final Four picks and bold calls on who cuts down the nets. No. 2 seed UConn and No. 15 seed Furman play Friday, March 20, in a March Madness NCAA Tournament first-round game . The East Region game is scheduled for 7 p.m. MST and can be seen on TBS ( stream with Sling TV ). Who will win the March Madness game and advance to the second round in the NCAA Tournament bracket ? Check out these March Madness picks and March Madness predictions for the men's college basketball NCAA Tournament matchup . UConn is a 20.5-point favorite over Furman in the game in March Madness odds provided by BetMGM Sportsbook . The Huskies are -5000 on the moneyline. The Paladins are +1450. The over/under for the game is set at 137.5 points. March Madness picks: Ohio State-TCU | Nebraska-Troy | Louisville-South Florida | Wisconsin-High Point | Duke-Siena | Vanderbilt-McNeese | Michigan State-North Dakota State | Arkansas-Hawaii | North Carolina-VCU | Saint Mary's-Texas A&M | Illinois-Penn | Georgia-Saint Louis | Gonzaga-Kennesaw State | Houston-Idaho | Kentucky-Santa Clara | Texas Tech-Akron | Arizona-Long Island | Virginia-Wright State | Iowa State-Tennessee State | Alabama-Hofstra | Villanova-Utah State | Clemson-Iowa | St. John's-UNI | UCLA-UCF | Purdue-Queens | Kansas-Cal Baptist | UConn-Furman | Miami-Missouri | The Republic's score predictions Watch UConn vs Furman on Sling The Arizona Republic : UConn 81, Furman 59 Jeremy Cluff writes: "UConn laid an egg in the Big East tournament championship game. Something tells us that Danny Hurley and the Huskies will use that as fuel in March Madness." March Madness picks: Predictions for NCAA Tournament first-round games Dimers : UConn 79, Furman 58 It writes: "After extensive simulations, our model gives Furman a win probability of 3%, while Connecticut has a win probability of 97%." modelTrace.content:
You are an analyst predicting a single March Madness game inside a bracket run.
You have live tools. Use them before making a pick when they can reduce uncertainty.
You are not filling the whole bracket in one response. Predict only the current game.
Use prior picks as already committed bracket state.
You may use at most 10 tool rounds before you must finalize your answer.
Before making a pick, investigate relevant context such as injuries or availability concerns, recent team news, prior head-to-head results when useful, and multiple raw statistics from the available ratings and matchup data.
Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.
Return strict JSON only.
Final JSON keys: pick, reasoningStep.
pick must contain gameId, winnerId, confidence, rationale.
The rationale must be a short paragraph of 2 to 4 sentences explaining why the winner was chosen.
reasoningStep must contain id, title, summary, evidence.
Bracket config id: 2026-bracket
Tournament year: 2026
Current game id: r64-east-8
Current game label: East 2/15
Round: Round of 64
Resolved teams: UConn (uconn) vs Furman (furman)
Prior committed picks: 11
